plan: Create a new CSS file `src/styles/responsive.css` with mobile breakpoints at 480px (small phones) and 600px (existing breakpoint to consolidate). This file will contain all responsive overrides for the generated site content, floating controls, info modal, and toast — keeping mobile rules centralized rather than scattered across individual CSS files.

Add responsive rules for floating controls (info-button, refresh-button, share-button): reduce `right` and `bottom` offsets from 24px to 16px at ≤480px to reclaim edge space on narrow screens, and adjust vertical stacking (`bottom` values) so buttons remain non-overlapping with tighter spacing (e.g., bottom: 16px / 64px / 112px instead of 24px / 80px / 136px).

Add responsive typography rules: set a minimum `font-size` of 14px on `#generated-site` at ≤480px, scale `.hero-tagline` down to `1.25em` at ≤480px (already 1.5em at ≤600px), and ensure `.site-body h2` scales to `1.25em` to remain readable without pinch-zoom.

Add responsive rules for the info modal: at ≤480px, set `.info-modal` to `width: 95%`, reduce padding from 32px to 20px, and ensure the modal doesn't overflow the viewport height with `max-height: 90vh; overflow-y: auto`.
Add responsive rules for the toast: at ≤480px, set `max-width: calc(100vw - 32px)` and `white-space: normal` to prevent horizontal overflow from long toast messages on narrow screens.

Add responsive rules for the roster table (guild roster module): at ≤480px, wrap in `overflow-x: auto` via a `.roster-table` parent rule to allow horizontal scrolling rather than causing page-level overflow. Also reduce table cell padding.
Add a global `max-width: 100vw; overflow-x: hidden` rule on `#generated-site` to prevent any horizontal overflow from content that might escape its container (defensive measure for 320px+ viewports).
Import `responsive.css` in `src/main.ts` alongside existing style imports — place it last so responsive overrides take precedence.

Consolidate the existing `@media (max-width: 600px)` block from `generated-site.css` into the new `responsive.css` file, removing it from `generated-site.css` to keep all breakpoint logic in one place.

Create test file `tests/us-016-responsive-mobile.test.ts` following the established test pattern (file existence checks, CSS rule assertions via regex). Test that: (a) `responsive.css` exists and is imported in `main.ts`, (b) media queries for ≤480px and ≤600px exist, (c) floating control responsive rules exist (bottom/right overrides), (d) info-modal responsive rules exist (max-height, overflow), (e) toast responsive rule prevents overflow (max-width), (f) `overflow-x: hidden` is present for horizontal scroll prevention, (g) TypeScript compiles cleanly, (h) Vite build succeeds.
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all acceptance criteria pass.
testing_recommendations: Verify no horizontal scrollbar appears at 320px, 375px, and 414px viewport widths by running Vite dev server and testing in browser DevTools responsive mode
Test floating controls remain tappable and non-overlapping at 320px width — all three buttons should be visible and have at least 44px touch targets with adequate spacing
Verify info modal is fully visible and scrollable on a 320px × 568px viewport (iPhone SE size) without content being cut off
Test toast messages with long text (e.g., 'Link copied to clipboard!') don't cause horizontal overflow at 320px
Verify the guild roster table scrolls horizontally within its container rather than causing page-level overflow at narrow widths
Test that hero tagline text wraps naturally and remains readable (≥14px effective size) without pinch zoom at 320px
Run the full test suite (`npm run test`) to confirm all existing tests still pass and new responsive tests pass
Run `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` to verify no regressions
risks: Moving the existing 600px media query from generated-site.css to responsive.css changes CSS specificity/cascade order — must ensure responsive.css is imported after generated-site.css in main.ts to maintain override behavior
Reducing floating control spacing at 480px could cause overlap if the toast appears at the same time as buttons — need to verify toast bottom position accounts for tighter button stacking

The `overflow-x: hidden` on #generated-site could clip intentionally wide content in certain era themes (e.g., early-web tables) — test with multiple seeds across eras to confirm no visual regressions

Existing tests in us-013/014/015 assert specific pixel values for button positioning (e.g., bottom: 24px, right: 24px) — responsive overrides won't break these since tests read the base CSS files, but worth verifying
